title: Helix Investor Research Report Walkthrough
description: Tour of the 10-section investor research report for Helix for ERPs pitch deck and website content

steps.title: Executive Summary
steps.description: Two-paragraph synthesis of the entire report: structural gap thesis, production traction metrics, market sizing, financial model highlights, and competitive positioning. Designed for the reader to grasp the full narrative in 60 seconds.

steps.title: Section 1: The Structural Gap
steps.description: The narrative spine of the entire pitch. Establishes the three-way gap (ERPs don't own, consultants don't persist, AI doesn't govern) and the macro trend of shrinking operating teams. Contains the compressed five-line thesis verbatim from Positioning Refined.

steps.title: Section 2: Market Opportunity
steps.description: Three concentric TAM rings ($600M conservative -> $2.4B-$9.6B mid -> $50.1B multi-ERP) with externally validated market data. Leads with the defensible floor per tech-research recommendation.

steps.title: Section 3: Capabilities at Highest Category Level
steps.description: Six capability categories anchored to Manifesto principles. Zero software implementation details per user guidance. Includes the boundary test and 'What Helix Is Not' distinction.

steps.title: Section 4: Traction with Honest Metrics
steps.description: Production metrics table with acceleration narrative, followed by proactive addressing of weak metrics (55% run success, 31% NS deployment). Demonstrates transparency that builds investor trust.

steps.title: Section 5: Three-Layer Financial Model
steps.description: Layer 1 (Dovie base case), Layer 2 (four stress-test vulnerabilities with framings), Layer 3 (pricing headroom and upside). All figures traceable to source PDFs.

steps.title: Section 6: Concentric Compression Model
steps.description: Competitive landscape structured as two forces compressing inward (Oracle from left, AI tools from right) with Helix in the ungoverned center. 'Capability is abundant, governance is scarce.'

steps.title: Section 7: Risk Register as Founder Discipline
steps.description: Reality Check milestone gates (3/6/12/18/36 months) with kill rules repositioned as execution accountability. Eight key risks with mitigations.

steps.title: Section 8: Team & Dual Investment Structure
steps.description: 7-person team table and two complementary investment tiers (Dovie angel + One-Pager seed) at shared $2.83M pre-money valuation.

steps.title: Section 9: Pitch-Ready Branding Language
steps.description: Context-organized lines ready for direct lift: homepage, pitch, one-slide thesis, manifesto hook, boundary test. All five tagline finalists with rankings.

steps.title: Section 10: Future Vision and Urgency
steps.description: ERP-as-database thesis, multi-ERP expansion (with honest acknowledgment of NetSuite-only readiness), pricing expansion headroom, and Reality Check closing urgency.

steps.title: Evidence Quality Framework
steps.description: Four-tier evidence quality system (A: hard facts, B: well-sourced external, C: projections, D: unverifiable) with source document catalog and claims requiring caution.
